x-enumeration-literals.name: Cash Dividend
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A dividend paid in cash directly to shareholders accounts.

Comment:
Example
A client holds 1,000 shares of a company paying €0.50 per share → Receives €500 cash dividend

x-enumeration-literals.name: Stock Dividend
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A dividend paid in additional shares instead of cash.

Comment:
Example
A 10% stock dividend → Investor with 1,000 shares receives 100 extra shares

x-enumeration-literals.name: Property Dividend
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A dividend paid in assets other than cash or shares, such as securities or physical assets.

Comment:
Example
A company distributes shares of a subsidiary → Shareholders receive new company shares instead of cash

x-enumeration-literals.name: Special Dividend
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A one-time, non-recurring dividend paid in addition to regular dividends.

Comment:
Company sells a business unit and pays €5 per share →
This is a special dividend

x-enumeration-literals.name: Interim Dividend
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A dividend paid before final annual profits are confirmed.

Comment:
Example
A company pays €0.30 mid-year → This is an interim dividend

x-enumeration-literals.name: Final Dividend
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A dividend declared after annual results are finalized.

Comment:
Example
After year-end results, company pays €0.80 → This is a final dividend

x-enumeration-literals.name: Dividend Reinvestment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A dividend where cash is automatically reinvested into additional shares.

Comment:
Example
€500 dividend is reinvested →Investor receives additional fractional shares
